name: Rome
localTips: Cappuccino is a breakfast drink — order espresso after 11am unless you enjoy gentle mockery.
Drink from the 'nasoni' — Rome's 2,500 cast-iron street fountains run free, cold spring water.
Restaurants that display photos of food or have staff beckoning you in are tourist traps — walk two streets further.
Churches are free museums: Caravaggios hang in San Luigi dei Francesi and Santa Maria del Popolo. Cover shoulders and knees.
The first Sunday of each month, state museums including the Colosseum are free — expect queues.

activities.title: Centro Storico evening passeggiata
activities.description: Pantheon to Piazza Navona to Campo de' Fiori — join the Roman evening stroll when the travertine glows and the fountains are lit.

activities.title: Trevi Fountain & coin toss
activities.description: Baroque theatre in marble and water — toss a coin over your left shoulder to guarantee a return to Rome, as tradition demands.

activities.title: The Pantheon
activities.description: Two thousand years old and still the world's largest unreinforced concrete dome — stand under the oculus as the light beam sweeps the floor.

activities.title: Gianicolo Hill at sunset
activities.description: The terrace above Trastevere serves Rome's finest skyline — domes, umbrella pines and swifts wheeling in golden light.

activities.title: Trastevere lanes wander
activities.description: Ochre walls, ivy, laundry lines and the mosaic-filled basilica of Santa Maria — Rome's village-within-the-city, best without a map.

activities.title: Villa Borghese gardens
activities.description: Rome's central park — rent a rowboat on the little lake, or just drift between the umbrella pines to the Pincio terrace view over Piazza del Popolo.

activities.title: Free Caravaggios in three churches
activities.description: San Luigi dei Francesi, Sant'Agostino and Santa Maria del Popolo hold six Caravaggio masterpieces between them — drop a euro in the light box.

activities.title: Aventine Hill: keyhole & orange garden
activities.description: Peer through the Knights of Malta keyhole to find St Peter's dome perfectly framed, then watch the sunset from the Giardino degli Aranci.

activities.title: Spanish Steps & the Tridente
activities.description: Climb the famous staircase early before the crowds, then window-shop the designer spine of Via Condotti down to Piazza del Popolo.

activities.title: Roman Forum from the free viewpoints
activities.description: Via del Campidoglio and the terrace behind the Capitoline give you the full Forum panorama without a ticket — magical at dusk when floodlit.

activities.title: Testaccio Market food stalls
activities.description: Rome's most authentic covered market — supplì, fresh pasta and the legendary allesso sandwich at Mordi e Vai, with zero tourist markup.

activities.title: Castel Sant'Angelo bridge & river walk
activities.description: Bernini's angels line the most beautiful bridge in Rome — walk the Tiber embankment from here toward St Peter's as the light fades.

activities.title: Basilica di Santa Maria Maggiore
activities.description: Fifth-century golden mosaics under a gilded ceiling — one of Rome's four papal basilicas, free and usually a fraction of the Vatican's crowds.

activities.title: Monti quarter boutiques & wine bars
activities.description: Rome's coolest little neighborhood — vintage shops, artisan studios and ivy-draped lanes squeezed between the Forum and Termini.

activities.title: Galleria Sciarra hidden courtyard
activities.description: Two minutes from the Trevi mob, an Art Nouveau courtyard covered in frescoes of 'virtuous women' that almost nobody enters.
activities.insiderNote: It's a working office building's passageway — free, open weekday business hours, and gloriously photogenic when shafts of light hit the frescoes.

activities.title: San Clemente's three layers underground
activities.description: Descend from a 12th-century basilica through a 4th-century church into a 1st-century Roman house and Mithraic temple — time travel by staircase.
activities.insiderNote: The underground levels are blissfully cool in summer and dry in rain — Rome's best bad-weather secret at a fraction of Colosseum prices.

activities.title: Quadraro street-art safari
activities.description: A working-class quarter turned open-air gallery — dozens of giant murals from the MURo project, and not a tour group in sight.
activities.insiderNote: Take Metro A to Porta Furba; grab a maritozzo (cream bun) from a local bar before you start walking.

activities.title: Aperitivo hour in Trastevere
activities.description: From 18:30 the bars around Piazza Trilussa pour spritzes with free snack spreads — Rome's happiest hour, riverside.
activities.insiderNote: Freni e Frizioni's aperitivo buffet is the local legend — one €10 drink buys you access to a full spread. Arrive by 19:00.

activities.title: Non-Catholic Cemetery & the Pyramid
activities.description: Keats and Shelley rest under cypresses beside an actual 2,000-year-old Roman pyramid, patrolled by the cemetery's famous cats.
activities.insiderNote: Entry is by donation (€3 suggested). Combine with Testaccio market two streets away for lunch.

activities.title: Proper gelato crawl
activities.description: Skip the neon mountains — hunt the real thing at Otaleg in Trastevere, Fatamorgana in Monti, and Gelateria dei Gracchi near the Vatican.
activities.insiderNote: Real gelato is stored in covered steel tins, not fluffed into peaks. Pistachio should be khaki-brown, never bright green.

activities.title: Historic caffès: Sant'Eustachio & Tazza d'Oro
activities.description: Two temples of Roman espresso a few streets apart near the Pantheon — order 'un caffè' at the bar and drink it standing like a local.
activities.insiderNote: At Sant'Eustachio the sugar is whipped in by default — ask for 'senza zucchero' if you want it straight. Tazza d'Oro's granita di caffè is the summer move.

activities.title: Colosseum, Forum & Palatine combined entry
activities.description: The amphitheatre of 50,000, then walk the Via Sacra through the Forum and up the Palatine where Rome was founded — one ticket, half a day.
activities.bookingQuery: Colosseum Roman Forum Palatine Hill entry ticket Rome

activities.title: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
activities.description: Nine miles of art ending under Michelangelo's ceiling — book the first slot or a late-afternoon entry to dodge the worst crush.
activities.bookingQuery: Vatican Museums Sistine Chapel skip the line ticket

activities.title: Galleria Borghese timed entry
activities.description: Bernini's marble that turns to flesh — Apollo and Daphne, David mid-sling — plus Caravaggios, in a villa of manageable size. Rome's best museum.
activities.bookingQuery: Galleria Borghese timed entry ticket Rome

activities.title: Pasta-making class with a Roman nonna
activities.description: Roll fettuccine and fold ravioli by hand, then eat your work with local wine — the souvenir skill that outlasts any fridge magnet.
activities.bookingQuery: pasta making class with wine Rome

activities.title: Trastevere evening food tour
activities.description: Supplì, porchetta, cacio e pepe and gelato across five family-run stops, with stories of the neighborhood between bites.
activities.bookingQuery: Trastevere evening street food tour Rome

activities.title: Catacombs & Appian Way tour
activities.description: Underground burial tunnels of the earliest Christians beneath the ancient Appian Way — cool, eerie and constant 15°C all year.
activities.bookingQuery: Rome catacombs and Appian Way guided tour

activities.title: Vespa sidecar tour
activities.description: Buzz between the seven hills in a vintage sidecar with a driver-guide — the 'Roman Holiday' fantasy, no licence required.

activities.title: Castel Sant'Angelo entry
activities.description: Hadrian's mausoleum turned papal fortress — spiral up the ancient ramp to a rooftop terrace with the best view of St Peter's.
activities.bookingQuery: Castel Sant'Angelo skip the line ticket Rome

activities.title: Tivoli: Villa d'Este & Hadrian's Villa day trip
activities.description: The Renaissance water-garden of a hundred fountains and the emperor's sprawling country palace, an hour east of Rome.
activities.bookingQuery: Tivoli Villa d'Este Hadrian's Villa day trip from Rome

activities.title: Christmas market on Piazza Navona
activities.description: Rome's beloved Befana market — nativity stalls, roasted chestnuts and carousel rides filling Bernini's great baroque square.

activities.title: Estate Romana: open-air cinema & river bars
activities.description: All summer the Tiber island hosts an open-air cinema and the riverbanks fill with pop-up bars — Rome's nights move outdoors.
